name: parsley
summary: Mechanism context: Apigenin/flavone-mediated GABA-A modulation, Nrf2 antioxidant signaling, PI3K/Akt signaling. Safety: Culinary use is usually low risk, but high-dose extracts/essential oil may be unsafe; avoid concentrated use in pregnancy, kidney disease, and with anticoagulants/warfarin-sensitive vitamin K management unless supervised.. Interactions tracked: Potential interaction concern with antihypertensives/diuretics, sedatives/CNS depressants, use clinician review for high-dose extracts or chronic use..

safetyNotes: Culinary use is usually low risk, but high-dose extracts/essential oil may be unsafe; avoid concentrated use in pregnancy, kidney disease, and with anticoagulants/warfarin-sensitive vitamin K management unless supervised.
contraindications: significant kidney disease or unstable electrolytes
interactions: use clinician review for high-dose extracts or chronic use.
preparation: Preparation varies by tradition and product form; food/tea use should not be treated as equivalent to concentrated extracts.
